NKJV.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.
NKJV.2: “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment with promise:
NKJV.3: “that it may be well with you and you may live long on the earth.”
NKJV.4: And you, fathers, do not provoke your children to wrath, but bring them up in the training and admonition of the Lord.
NKJV.5: Bondservants, be obedient to those who are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in sincerity of heart, as to Christ;
NKJV.6: not with eyeservice, as men-pleasers, but as bondservants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart,
NKJV.7: with goodwill doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men,
NKJV.8: knowing that whatever good anyone does, he will receive the same from the Lord, whether he is a slave or free.
NKJV.9: And you, masters, do the same things to them, giving up threatening, knowing that your own Master also is in heaven, and there is no partiality with Him.
NKJV.10: Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord and in the power of His might.
NKJV.11: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
NKJV.12: For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this age, against spiritual hosts of wickedness in the heavenly places.
NKJV.13: Therefore take up the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.
NKJV.14: Stand therefore, having girded your waist with truth, having put on the breastplate of righteousness,
NKJV.15: and having shod your feet with the preparation of the gospel of peace;
NKJV.16: above all, taking the shield of faith with which you will be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked one.
NKJV.17: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God;
NKJV.18: praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, being watchful to this end with all perseverance and supplication for all the saints—
NKJV.19: and for me, that utterance may be given to me, that I may open my mouth boldly to make known the mystery of the gospel,
NKJV.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains; that in it I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.
NKJV.21: But that you also may know my affairs and how I am doing, Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, will make all things known to you;
NKJV.22: whom I have sent to you for this very purpose, that you may know our affairs, and that he may comfort your hearts.
NKJV.23: Peace to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.24: Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.

ESV.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.
ESV.2: “Honor your father and mother” (this is the first commandment with a promise),
ESV.3: “that it may go well with you and that you may live long in the land.”
ESV.4: Fathers, do not provoke your children to anger, but bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
ESV.5: Bondservants,  obey your earthly masters  with fear and trembling, with a sincere heart, as you would Christ,
ESV.6: not by the way of eye-service, as people-pleasers, but as bondservants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart,
ESV.7: rendering service with a good will as to the Lord and not to man,
ESV.8: knowing that whatever good anyone does, this he will receive back from the Lord, whether he is a bondservant or is free.
ESV.9: Masters, do the same to them, and stop your threatening, knowing that he who is both their Master  and yours is in heaven, and that there is no partiality with him.
ESV.10: Finally, be strong in the Lord and in the strength of his might.
ESV.11: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.
ESV.12: For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the cosmic powers over this present darkness, against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly places.
ESV.13: Therefore take up the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand firm.
ESV.14: Stand therefore, having fastened on the belt of truth, and having put on the breastplate of righteousness,
ESV.15: and, as shoes for your feet, having put on the readiness given by the gospel of peace.
ESV.16: In all circumstances take up the shield of faith, with which you can extinguish all the flaming darts of the evil one;
ESV.17: and take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God,
ESV.18: praying at all times in the Spirit, with all prayer and supplication. To that end, keep alert with all perseverance, making supplication for all the saints,
ESV.19: and also for me, that words may be given to me in opening my mouth boldly to proclaim the mystery of the gospel,
ESV.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains, that I may declare it boldly, as I ought to speak.
ESV.21: So that you also may know how I am and what I am doing, Tychicus the beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord will tell you everything.
ESV.22: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, that you may know how we are, and that he may encourage your hearts.
ESV.23: Peace be to the brothers,  and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.24: Grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with love incorruptible.

NASB.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.
NASB.2: HONOR YOUR FATHER AND MOTHER (which is the first commandment with a promise),
NASB.3: SO THAT IT MAY BE WELL WITH YOU, AND THAT YOU MAY LIVE LONG ON THE EARTH.
NASB.4: Fathers, do not provoke your children to anger, but bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
NASB.5: Slaves, be obedient to those who are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in the sincerity of your heart, as to Christ;
NASB.6: not by way of eyeservice, as men-pleasers, but as slaves of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart.
NASB.7: With good will render service, as to the Lord, and not to men,
NASB.8: knowing that whatever good thing each one does, this he will receive back from the Lord, whether slave or free.
NASB.9: And masters, do the same things to them, and give up threatening, knowing that both their Master and yours is in heaven, and there is no partiality with Him.
NASB.10: Finally, be strong in the Lord and in the strength of His might.
NASB.11: Put on the full armor of God, so that you will be able to stand firm against the schemes of the devil.
NASB.12: For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the powers, against the world forces of this darkness, against the spiritual [forces] of wickedness in the heavenly [places].
NASB.13: Therefore, take up the full armor of God, so that you will be able to resist in the evil day, and having done everything, to stand firm.
NASB.14: Stand firm therefore, HAVING GIRDED YOUR LOINS WITH TRUTH, and HAVING PUT ON THE BREASTPLATE OF RIGHTEOUSNESS,
NASB.15: and having shod YOUR FEET WITH THE PREPARATION OF THE GOSPEL OF PEACE;
NASB.16: in addition to all, taking up the shield of faith with which you will be able to extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil [one].
NASB.17: And take THE HELMET OF SALVATION, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
NASB.18: With all prayer and petition pray at all times in the Spirit, and with this in view, be on the alert with all perseverance and petition for all the saints,
NASB.19: and [pray] on my behalf, that utterance may be given to me in the opening of my mouth, to make known with boldness the mystery of the gospel,
NASB.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains; that in [proclaiming] it I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.
NASB.21: But that you also may know about my circumstances, how I am doing, Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, will make everything known to you.
NASB.22: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, so that you may know about us, and that he may comfort your hearts.
NASB.23: Peace be to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.24: Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ with incorruptible [love].

NLT.1: Children, obey your parents because you belong to the Lord, for this is the right thing to do.
NLT.2: “Honor your father and mother.” This is the first commandment with a promise:
NLT.3: If you honor your father and mother, “things will go well for you, and you will have a long life on the earth.”
NLT.4: Fathers, do not provoke your children to anger by the way you treat them. Rather, bring them up with the discipline and instruction that comes from the Lord.
NLT.5: Slaves, obey your earthly masters with deep respect and fear. Serve them sincerely as you would serve Christ.
NLT.6: Try to please them all the time, not just when they are watching you. As slaves of Christ, do the will of God with all your heart.
NLT.7: Work with enthusiasm, as though you were working for the Lord rather than for people.
NLT.8: Remember that the Lord will reward each one of us for the good we do, whether we are slaves or free.
NLT.9: Masters, treat your slaves in the same way. Don’t threaten them; remember, you both have the same Master in heaven, and he has no favorites.
NLT.10: A final word: Be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power.
NLT.11: Put on all of God’s armor so that you will be able to stand firm against all strategies of the devil.
NLT.12: For we are not fighting against flesh-and-blood enemies, but against evil rulers and authorities of the unseen world, against mighty powers in this dark world, and against evil spirits in the heavenly places.
NLT.13: Therefore, put on every piece of God’s armor so you will be able to resist the enemy in the time of evil. Then after the battle you will still be standing firm.
NLT.14: Stand your ground, putting on the belt of truth and the body armor of God’s righteousness.
NLT.15: For shoes, put on the peace that comes from the Good News so that you will be fully prepared.
NLT.16: In addition to all of these, hold up the shield of faith to stop the fiery arrows of the devil.
NLT.17: Put on salvation as your helmet, and take the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
NLT.18: Pray in the Spirit at all times and on every occasion. Stay alert and be persistent in your prayers for all believers everywhere.
NLT.19: And pray for me, too. Ask God to give me the right words so I can boldly explain God’s mysterious plan that the Good News is for Jews and Gentiles alike.
NLT.20: I am in chains now, still preaching this message as God’s ambassador. So pray that I will keep on speaking boldly for him, as I should.
NLT.21: To bring you up to date, Tychicus will give you a full report about what I am doing and how I am getting along. He is a beloved brother and faithful helper in the Lord’s work.
NLT.22: I have sent him to you for this very purpose — to let you know how we are doing and to encourage you.
NLT.23: Peace be with you, dear brothers and sisters, and may God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you love with faithfulness.
NLT.24: May God’s grace be eternally upon all who love our Lord Jesus Christ.

WEB.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.
WEB.2: “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment with a promise:
WEB.3: “that it may be well with you, and you may live long on the earth.”
WEB.4: You fathers, don’t provoke your children to wrath, but nurture them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
WEB.5: Servants, be obedient to those who according to the flesh are your masters, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as to Christ;
WEB.6: not in the way of service only when eyes are on you, as men pleasers; but as servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart;
WEB.7: with good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men;
WEB.8: knowing that whatever good thing each one does, he will receive the same again from the Lord, whether he is bound or free.
WEB.9: You masters, do the same things to them, and give up threatening, knowing that he who is both their Master and yours is in heaven, and there is no partiality with him.
WEB.10: Finally, be strong in the Lord, and in the strength of his might.
WEB.11: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
WEB.12: For our wrestling is not against flesh and blood, but against the principalities, against the powers, against the world’s rulers of the darkness of this age, and against the spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly places.
WEB.13: Therefore put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and, having done all, to stand.
WEB.14: Stand therefore, having the utility belt of truth buckled around your waist, and having put on the breastplate of righteousness,
WEB.15: and having fitted your feet with the preparation of the Good News of peace;
WEB.16: above all, taking up the shield of faith, with which you will be able to quench all the fiery darts of the evil one.
WEB.17: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word  of God;
WEB.18: with all prayer and requests, praying at all times in the Spirit, and being watchful to this end in all perseverance and requests for all the saints:
WEB.19: on my behalf, that utterance may be given to me in opening my mouth, to make known with boldness the mystery of the Good News,
WEB.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains; that in it I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.
WEB.21: But that you also may know my affairs, how I am doing, Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make known to you all things;
WEB.22: whom I have sent to you for this very purpose, that you may know our state, and that he may comfort your hearts.
WEB.23: Peace be to the brothers, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.24: Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ with incorruptible love. Amen.

CSB17.1: Children, obey <sup>ⓘ</sup> your parents in the Lord, because this is right.
CSB17.2: Honor your father and mother, which is the first commandment with a promise,
CSB17.3: so that it may go well with you and that you may have a long life in the land. [1] [2] ⓙ
CSB17.4: Fathers, don’t stir up anger <sup>ⓚ</sup> in your children, but bring them up in the training <sup>ⓛ</sup> and instruction of the Lord.
CSB17.5: Slaves, obey your human <sup>[3]</sup> masters <sup>ⓜ</sup> with fear and trembling, <sup>ⓝ</sup> in the sincerity <sup>ⓐ</sup> of your heart, as you would Christ.
CSB17.6: Don’t work only while being watched, as people-pleasers, but as slaves of Christ, do God’s will <sup>ⓑ</sup> from your heart.
CSB17.7: Serve with a good attitude, as to the Lord and not to people, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.8: knowing that whatever good each one does, slave or free, he will receive this back from the Lord.
CSB17.9: And masters, treat your slaves the same way, without threatening <sup>ⓓ</sup> them, because you know that both their Master and yours is in heaven, and there is no favoritism <sup>ⓔ</sup> with him. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.10: Finally, be strengthened by the Lord and by his vast strength. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.11: Put on <sup>ⓗ</sup> the full armor <sup>ⓘ</sup> of God so that you can stand against the schemes of the devil.
CSB17.12: For our struggle is not against flesh <sup>ⓙ</sup> and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, <sup>ⓚ</sup> against the cosmic powers of this darkness, against evil, <sup>ⓛ</sup> spiritual forces in the heavens. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.13: For this reason take up the full armor <sup>ⓝ</sup> of God, so that you may be able to resist <sup>ⓐ</sup> in the evil day, and having prepared everything, to take your stand.
CSB17.14: Stand, <sup>ⓑ</sup> therefore, with truth <sup>ⓒ</sup> like a belt around your waist, righteousness <sup>ⓓ</sup> like armor on your chest, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.15: and your feet sandaled with readiness for the gospel of peace.
CSB17.16: In every situation take up the shield <sup>ⓕ</sup> of faith <sup>ⓖ</sup> with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: Take the helmet <sup>ⓘ</sup> of salvation and the sword <sup>ⓙ</sup> of the Spirit <sup>ⓚ</sup>  — which is the word of God.
CSB17.18: Pray <sup>ⓛ</sup> at all times in the Spirit <sup>ⓜ</sup> with every prayer and request, and stay alert <sup>ⓝ</sup> with all perseverance and intercession for all the saints.
CSB17.19: Pray also for me, that the message may be given to me when I open my mouth to make known with boldness <sup>ⓐ</sup> the mystery of the gospel.
CSB17.20: For this I am an ambassador <sup>ⓑ</sup> in chains. Pray that I might be bold enough to speak about it as I should.
CSB17.21: Tychicus, <sup>ⓒ</sup> our dearly loved brother and faithful servant <sup>[4]</sup> in the Lord, will tell you all the news about me so that you may be informed.
CSB17.22: I am sending him to you for this very reason, to let you know how we are and to encourage <sup>ⓓ</sup> your hearts. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.23: Peace to the brothers and sisters, and love with faith, from God the Father <sup>ⓕ</sup> and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.24: Grace be with all who have undying love for our Lord Jesus Christ. <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>[6]</sup>

AMP.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord [that is, accept their guidance and discipline as His representatives], for this is right [for obedience teaches wisdom and self-discipline].
AMP.2: Honor [esteem, value as precious] your father and your mother [and be respectful to them]—this is the first commandment with a promise—
AMP.3: <e>so that it may be well with you, and that you may have a long life on the earth</e>.
AMP.4: Fathers, do not provoke your children to anger [do not exasperate them to the point of resentment with demands that are trivial or unreasonable or humiliating or abusive; nor by showing favoritism or indifference to any of them], but bring them up [tenderly, with lovingkindness] in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
AMP.5: Slaves, be obedient to those who are your earthly masters, with respect for authority, and with a sincere heart [seeking to please them], as [service] to Christ—
AMP.6: not in the way of eye-service [working only when someone is watching you and only] to please men, but as slaves of Christ, doing the will of God from your heart;
AMP.7: rendering service with goodwill, as to the Lord, and not [only] to men,
AMP.8: knowing that whatever good thing each one does, he will receive this back from the Lord, whether [he is] slave or free.
AMP.9: You masters, do the same [showing goodwill] toward them, and give up threatening and abusive words, knowing that [He who is] both their true Master and yours is in heaven, and that there is no partiality with Him [regardless of one's earthly status].
AMP.10: In conclusion, be strong in the Lord [draw your strength from Him and be empowered through your union with Him] and in the power of His [boundless] might.
AMP.11: Put on the full armor of God [for His precepts are like the splendid armor of a heavily-armed soldier], so that you may be able to [successfully] stand up against all the schemes and the strategies and the deceits of the devil.
AMP.12: For our struggle is not against flesh and blood [contending only with physical opponents], but against the rulers, against the powers, against the world forces of this [present] darkness, against the spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly (supernatural) places.
AMP.13: Therefore, put on the complete armor of God, so that you will be able to [successfully] resist and stand your ground in the evil day [of danger], and having done everything [that the crisis demands], to stand firm [in your place, fully prepared, immovable, victorious].
AMP.14: So stand firm and hold your ground, havingtightened the wide band of truth (personal integrity, moral courage) around your waist and having put on the breastplate of righteousness (an upright heart),
AMP.15: and having strapped on your feet the gospel of peace in preparation [to face the enemy with firm-footed stability and the readiness produced by the good news].
AMP.16: Above all, lift up the [protective] shield of faith with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
AMP.17: And take <e>the helmet of salvation</e>, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the Word of God.
AMP.18: With all prayer and petition pray [with specific requests] at all times [on every occasion and in every season] in the Spirit, and with this in view, stay alert with all perseverance and petition [interceding in prayer] for all God's people.
AMP.19: And pray for me, that words may be given to me when I open my mouth, to proclaim boldly the mystery of the good news [of salvation],
AMP.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains. And pray that in proclaiming it I may speak boldly and courageously, as I should.
AMP.21: Now, so that you may know how I am and what I am doing, Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, will tell you everything.
AMP.22: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, so that you may know how we are, and that he may comfort and encourage and strengthen your hearts.
AMP.23: Peace be to the brothers and sisters, and love joined with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.24: Grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with undying and incorruptible love.

MSG.1: Children, do what your parents tell you. This is only right.
MSG.2: "Honor your father and mother" is the first commandment that has a promise attached to it, namely,
MSG.3: "so you will live well and have a long life."
MSG.4: Fathers, don't exasperate your children by coming down hard on them. Take them by the hand and lead them in the way of the Master.
MSG.5: Servants, respectfully obey your earthly masters but always with an eye to obeying the real master, Christ.
MSG.6: Don't just do what you have to do to get by, but work heartily, as Christ's servants doing what God wants you to do.
MSG.7: And work with a smile on your face, always keeping in mind that no matter who happens to be giving the orders, you're really serving God.
MSG.8: Good work will get you good pay from the Master, regardless of whether you are slave or free.
MSG.9: Masters, it's the same with you. No abuse, please, and no threats. You and your servants are both under the same Master in heaven. He makes no distinction between you and them.
MSG.10: And that about wraps it up. God is strong, and he wants you strong.
MSG.11: So take everything the Master has set out for you, well-made weapons of the best materials. And put them to use so you will be able to stand up to everything the Devil throws your way.
MSG.12: This is no afternoon athletic contest that we'll walk away from and forget about in a couple of hours. This is for keeps, a life-or-death fight to the finish against the Devil and all his angels.
MSG.13: Be prepared. You're up against far more than you can handle on your own. Take all the help you can get, every weapon God has issued, so that when it's all over but the shouting you'll still be on your feet.
MSG.17: and salvation are more than words. Learn how to apply them. You'll need them throughout your life. God's Word is an indispensable weapon.
MSG.18: In the same way, prayer is essential in this ongoing warfare. Pray hard and long. Pray for your brothers and sisters. Keep your eyes open. Keep each other's spirits up so that no one falls behind or drops out.
MSG.19: And don't forget to pray for me. Pray that I'll know what to say and have the courage to say it at the right time, telling the mystery to one and all, the Message that I,
MSG.20: jailbird preacher that I am, am responsible for getting out.
MSG.21: Tychicus, my good friend here, will tell you what I'm doing and how things are going with me. He is certainly a dependable servant of the Master!
MSG.22: I've sent him not only to tell you about us but to cheer you on in your faith.
MSG.23: Good-bye, friends. Love mixed with faith be yours from God the Father and from the Master, Jesus Christ.
MSG.24: Pure grace and nothing but grace be with all who love our Master, Jesus Christ.

NIV.1:
Children and Parents
Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.
NIV.2: “Honor your father and mother”—which is the first commandment with a promise—
NIV.3: “that it may go well with you and that you may enjoy long life on the earth.”
NIV.4: Fathers, do not exasperate your children; instead, bring them up in the training and instruction of the Lord.
NIV.5:
Slaves and Masters
Slaves, obey your earthly masters with respect and fear, and with sincerity of heart, just as you would obey Christ.
NIV.6: Obey them not only to win their favor when their eye is on you, but like slaves of Christ, doing the will of God from your heart.
NIV.7: Serve wholeheartedly, as if you were serving the Lord, not men,
NIV.8: because you know that the Lord will reward everyone for whatever good he does, whether he is slave or free.
NIV.9: And masters, treat your slaves in the same way. Do not threaten them, since you know that he who is both their Master and yours is in heaven, and there is no favoritism with him.
NIV.10:
The Armor of God
Finally, be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power.
NIV.11: Put on the full armor of God so that you can take your stand against the devil’s schemes.
NIV.12: For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.
NIV.13: Therefore put on the full armor of God, so that when the day of evil comes, you may be able to stand your ground, and after you have done everything, to stand.
NIV.14: Stand firm then, with the belt of truth buckled around your waist, with the breastplate of righteousness in place,
NIV.15: and with your feet fitted with the readiness that comes from the gospel of peace.
NIV.16: In addition to all this, take up the shield of faith, with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
NIV.17: Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
NIV.18: And pray in the Spirit on all occasions with all kinds of prayers and requests. With this in mind, be alert and always keep on praying for all the saints.
NIV.19: Pray also for me, that whenever I open my mouth, words may be given me so that I will fearlessly make known the mystery of the gospel,
NIV.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains. Pray that I may declare it fearlessly, as I should.
NIV.21:
Final Greetings
Tychicus, the dear brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will tell you everything, so that you also may know how I am and what I am doing.
NIV.22: I am sending him to you for this very purpose, that you may know how we are, and that he may encourage you.
NIV.23: Peace to the brothers, and love with faith from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.24: Grace to all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with an undying love.

NRSVCE.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.
NRSVCE.2: “Honor your father and mother” — this is the first commandment with a promise:
NRSVCE.3: “so that it may be well with you and you may live long on the earth.”
NRSVCE.4: And, fathers, do not provoke your children to anger, but bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
NRSVCE.5: Slaves, obey your earthly masters with fear and trembling, in singleness of heart, as you obey Christ;
NRSVCE.6: not only while being watched, and in order to please them, but as slaves of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart.
NRSVCE.7: Render service with enthusiasm, as to the Lord and not to men and women,
NRSVCE.8: knowing that whatever good we do, we will receive the same again from the Lord, whether we are slaves or free.
NRSVCE.9: And, masters, do the same to them. Stop threatening them, for you know that both of you have the same Master in heaven, and with him there is no partiality.
NRSVCE.10: Finally, be strong in the Lord and in the strength of his power.
NRSVCE.11: Put on the whole armor of God, so that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
NRSVCE.12: For our struggle is not against enemies of blood and flesh, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the cosmic powers of this present darkness, against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly places.
NRSVCE.13: Therefore take up the whole armor of God, so that you may be able to withstand on that evil day, and having done everything, to stand firm.
NRSVCE.14: Stand therefore, and fasten the belt of truth around your waist, and put on the breastplate of righteousness.
NRSVCE.15: As shoes for your feet put on whatever will make you ready to proclaim the gospel of peace.
NRSVCE.16: With all of these, take the shield of faith, with which you will be able to quench all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
NRSVCE.17: Take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
NRSVCE.18: Pray in the Spirit at all times in every prayer and supplication. To that end keep alert and always persevere in supplication for all the saints.
NRSVCE.19: Pray also for me, so that when I speak, a message may be given to me to make known with boldness the mystery of the gospel,
NRSVCE.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains. Pray that I may declare it boldly, as I must speak.
NRSVCE.21: So that you also may know how I am and what I am doing, Tychicus will tell you everything. He is a dear brother and a faithful minister in the Lord.
NRSVCE.22: I am sending him to you for this very purpose, to let you know how we are, and to encourage your hearts.
NRSVCE.23: Peace be to the whole community, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.24: Grace be with all who have an undying love for our Lord Jesus Christ.

NET.1: Children, obey your parents in the Lord for this is right.
NET.2: “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment accompanied by a promise, namely,
NET.3: “that it may go well with you and that you will live a long time on the earth.”
NET.4: Fathers, do not provoke your children to anger, but raise them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
NET.5: Slaves, obey your human masters with fear and trembling, in the sincerity of your heart as to Christ,
NET.6: not like those who do their work only when someone is watching — as people-pleasers — but as slaves of Christ doing the will of God from the heart.
NET.7: Obey with enthusiasm, as though serving the Lord and not people,
NET.8: because you know that each person, whether slave or free, if he does something good, this will be rewarded by the Lord.
NET.9: Masters, treat your slaves the same way, giving up the use of threats, because you know that both you and they have the same master in heaven, and there is no favoritism with him.
NET.10: Finally, be strengthened in the Lord and in the strength of his power.
NET.11: Clothe yourselves with the full armor of God so that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.
NET.12: For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the powers, against the world rulers of this darkness, against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavens.
NET.13: For this reason, take up the full armor of God so that you may be able to stand your ground on the evil day, and having done everything, to stand.
NET.14: Stand firm therefore, by fastening the belt of truth around your waist, by putting on the breastplate of righteousness,
NET.15: by fitting your feet with the preparation that comes from the good news of peace,
NET.16: and in all of this, by taking up the shield of faith with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
NET.17: And take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
NET.18: With every prayer and petition, pray at all times in the Spirit, and to this end be alert, with all perseverance and requests for all the saints.
NET.19: Pray for me also, that I may be given the message when I begin to speak — that I may confidently make known the mystery of the gospel,
NET.20: for which I am an ambassador in chains. Pray that I may be able to speak boldly as I ought to speak.
NET.21: Tychicus, my dear brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make everything known to you, so that you too may know about my circumstances, how I am doing.
NET.22: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, that you may know our circumstances and that he may encourage your hearts.
NET.23: Peace to the brothers and sisters, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.24: Grace be with all of those who love our Lord Jesus Christ with an undying love.
